notes: Famous published charts used as golden references. Ratings follow Astro-Databank (AA = birth certificate/record, A = memory/quoted).
Pre-timezone-era Indian/European births use Local Mean Time: tz_offset = longitude/15 unless a civil standard applied.
Degree-level checks are done with the ayanamsa the published chart used (B.V. Raman's charts -> 'raman'); sign-level sanity checks are repeated with Lahiri.

Einstein: the published Raman-ayanamsa chart has Moon in Scorpio. The AA-rated tropical Moon is 14deg31 Sagittarius; minus the Raman ayanamsa (~20.7deg in 1879) that is Scorpio ~23.8, which is what we assert. (An earlier draft of this task quoted Scorpio 13.8 — a 10-degree transcription slip inconsistent with the AA tropical position.)

Nehru: sign-level only (birth time A-rated). His famous chart is Cancer lagna / Cancer Moon under Lahiri. Under Raman the lagna computes to Leo 0.3 — right on the cusp — so the sign-level check is asserted under Lahiri, per the chart's own convention.

Bose: Cuttack 1897 ran on Madras Time (UTC+5:21:10 = 5.3528h). Both Madras Time and LMT (5.7222h) yield Aries lagna and Uttara Phalguni Moon; Madras Time is used as the historically correct civil standard.
